name: Cal.com MCP Server — Open Source Scheduling
what_it_does: MCP server for Cal.com — the open-source Calendly alternative. Enables AI agents to manage scheduling via Cal.com — creating bookings, checking availability, managing event types, and interacting with the Cal.com scheduling platform. Both self-hosted and Cal.com Cloud supported.
use_cases: Creating and managing meeting bookings from executive assistant agents
Checking calendar availability before scheduling from coordination agents
Managing event types and booking pages from scheduling automation agents
Building AI-powered booking flows using Cal.com as the scheduling backend
not_for: Teams using Calendly, Acuity, or other scheduling platforms (Cal.com specific)
Enterprise scheduling requiring custom CRM integrations not in Cal.com
best_when: A professional or team using Cal.com for scheduling wants AI agents to handle booking management — checking availability, creating bookings, and managing their scheduling calendar through Cal.com's API.
avoid_when: You use Calendly or other scheduling platforms. Cal.com's self-hosted option requires infrastructure management. Also consider privacy implications of AI scheduling access.

auth.notes: Cal.com API keys for personal use, OAuth for third-party integrations. Self-hosted instances use local API keys. Cal.com Cloud uses managed auth.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Cal.com free tier available; self-hosted is fully free
pricing.notes: Cal.com is open source — fully free to self-host. Cal.com Cloud has free and paid tiers. MCP integration is free.

performance.rate_limits: Cal.com API rate limits apply; generous for standard use

known_agent_gotchas: PRIVACY: Scheduling data reveals professional meeting patterns and contact relationships
Double-booking risk: ensure agents check availability before creating bookings
Time zone handling is critical for international scheduling — always specify timezone
Buffer times and busy periods must be respected — agents should not create back-to-back bookings
Webhook-based status updates recommended for async booking confirmation
agent_readiness.security_notes: PRIVACY: Meeting schedules reveal professional activities and contacts. OAuth with scoped access. Self-hosted option for complete data control. GDPR compliant.

security_score_components.security_notes: Scheduling data is personal professional information. GDPR compliant. Self-hosted option for data control. OAuth scoped access recommended.
